skill.name: gws-calendar-agenda
skill.description: Shows upcoming Google Calendar events across all calendars with date, calendar, and timezone filters.
skill.summary: View a read-only agenda from Google Calendar.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown command examples, relative documentation links, and weak-crypto keyword matches. Review found no malicious intent, no prompt injection, and no data exfiltration pattern in SKILL.md. The skill is read-only but depends on the local gws CLI and Google Workspace authentication.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported shell execution because SKILL.md uses backticks and fenced bash examples. These lines document intended gws CLI usage and do not include dynamic command construction, shell metacharacter injection, destructive commands, or hidden execution logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is Markdown documentation for a CLI skill, not executable Ruby or shell code embedded in a script. The examples use fixed gws commands with user-supplied flags limited to documented calendar filters.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported path traversal for relative links to shared skill documentation. The paths point to adjacent SKILL.md files for documentation and do not request arbitrary file reads or writes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The '..' sequences are static relative documentation references inside a skill collection. No variable path input, recursive traversal, or sensitive filesystem target is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography on lines containing the word description. No cryptographic function, cipher configuration, hashing operation, or credential handling code appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected lines are metadata and a Markdown table header. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or any other cryptographic algorithm usage.

content.value_statement: Calendar planning often requires checking several calendars before committing to work. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code request a read-only Google Calendar agenda through the gws CLI.
actual_capabilities: Shows upcoming events across all Google calendars by default.
Filters agenda output to today, tomorrow, this week, or a custom day count.
Filters results to a specific calendar name or calendar ID.
Supports an IANA timezone override for agenda display.
Documents read-only behavior for viewing events without modifying calendars.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Depends on access granted to the connected Google account.
Does not create, update, delete, or RSVP to calendar events.
Shared authentication and global flag guidance is stored in an adjacent skill file.

use_cases.title: Daily Schedule Review
use_cases.description: Check today's calendar events before planning work or meetings.

use_cases.title: Team Calendar Triage
use_cases.description: Filter a work calendar to see near-term meetings and deadlines.

use_cases.title: Travel Timezone Planning
use_cases.description: View upcoming events in a specific IANA timezone before travel.

prompt_templates.title: Show Today's Agenda
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my Google Calendar agenda for today.

prompt_templates.title: Check Tomorrow
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my Google Calendar events for tomorrow and highlight any back-to-back meetings.

prompt_templates.title: Review Work Calendar
prompt_templates.prompt: Show the next three days from my Work calendar and summarize the busiest periods.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Across Timezones
prompt_templates.prompt: Show this week's agenda using the America/New_York timezone and identify meetings outside normal business hours.

output_examples.output: A dated list of today's calendar events, including times, titles, and source calendars when available.

output_examples.input: Show the next three days for my Work calendar.
output_examples.output: A filtered agenda for the Work calendar with events grouped by day and summarized for planning.
A weekly agenda displayed in the requested timezone, with timing conflicts or busy blocks noted.

best_practices: Confirm the gws CLI is authenticated before requesting calendar data.
Use --calendar when you only need one calendar and want less output.
Use --timezone when reviewing events for travel or distributed teams.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for event changes because it is read-only.
Do not request unrelated Google Workspace data through this agenda command.
Do not assume all calendars are visible if the account lacks permissions.

faq.question: Does this skill modify Google Calendar events?
faq.answer: No. The skill documents read-only agenda commands and does not modify events.

faq.question: What tool must be installed?
faq.answer: The local gws CLI must be installed and authenticated with Google Workspace access.

faq.question: Can it show only one calendar?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the calendar filter with a calendar name or calendar ID.

faq.question: Can it show a weekly agenda?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented week flag shows events for the current week.

faq.question: Can it use a different timezone?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide an IANA timezone such as America/New_York.

faq.question: Does it work without Google account access?
faq.answer: No. It depends on the Google account and permissions configured for gws.
